title: EBUS 2026 Pre-Test
summary: Baseline 25-question assessment from the updated 2026 pre-course test set. Scores are recorded before the practice modules and lecture sequence unlock.
instructions: Complete the full pre-test after the welcome and course introduction video.
Correct answers are intentionally hidden during the pre-test experience.
Your saved score unlocks the practice modules and the next course video.
demoPolicy: This educational pre-test records score and completion status for course progression. It is not a diagnostic instrument.

questions.prompt: A 72-year-old man with a drug-eluting coronary stent placed 6 weeks ago has a positron emission tomography-avid subcarinal lymph node that requires tissue confirmation. He is taking aspirin and clopidogrel. Which preparation plan is most appropriate?

options.label: Continue aspirin; clopidogrel may be continued after cardiology input if thrombosis risk exceeds bleeding risk
Bridge with low-molecular-weight heparin and stop both medications

questions.prompt: While imaging a right paratracheal lymph node, multiple bright parallel lines suddenly appear on the ultrasound image. What is the best next step?

options.label: Increase suction through the working channel
Adjust the saline-filled balloon to improve contact with the airway wall
Advance the needle to stabilize the scope

questions.prompt: A patient with a left upper lobe lung cancer has a suspicious lymph node located along the left lateral tracheal wall above the superior border of the aortic arch and below the thoracic inlet. Which lymph node station is most consistent with this description?
Before puncturing a small hilar structure, ultrasound is used to determine the direction of blood flow relative to the transducer. Which ultrasound mode is being used?
During staging, rapid on-site evaluation from a lymph node shows malignant cells. What is the best next step?

options.label: Obtain additional passes for cell block preparation
Continue obtaining samples only for on-site evaluation

questions.prompt: A patient presents eight days after a bronchoscopic procedure with fever, pleuritic chest pain, and leukocytosis. Imaging shows subtle widening of the mediastinum. What is the most likely diagnosis?
A novice bronchoscopist has difficulty maneuvering the endobronchial ultrasound scope compared with a conventional bronchoscope. What is the primary reason?

options.label: The scope has a larger diameter
The scope scans in a full circular pattern
The endoscopic view is forward-oblique rather than straight ahead
The working channel is too small

questions.prompt: Immediately after needle puncture of a lymph node, the ultrasound image disappears due to loss of contact. What is the next step?

options.label: Stabilize the needle and re-anchor the scope against the airway wall

questions.prompt: A patient requires both mediastinal lymph node sampling and peripheral lung lesion biopsy without rapid on-site evaluation. What is the most appropriate anesthesia approach?

options.label: Moderate sedation for the entire procedure
Moderate sedation first with escalation if needed
Deep sedation or general anesthesia for the entire procedure
Deep sedation first with later de-escalation

questions.prompt: A patient undergoing combined bronchoscopy with aspiration risk requires airway management. What is the most appropriate airway approach?
A patient is undergoing a combined diagnostic procedure with rapid on-site evaluation available. What is the best sequence?
Which set of lymph node stations is routinely accessible using endobronchial ultrasound from the airway?
Cytologic analysis shows small mature lymphocytes with benign bronchial epithelial contamination. What is the best interpretation?
A tumor demonstrates nuclear molding, high proliferation index, and neuroendocrine markers. What is the most likely diagnosis?
Granulomatous inflammation is seen on initial evaluation. What is the best specimen handling strategy?

options.label: Place in lymphocyte preservation medium only
Divide material for formalin and saline processing

questions.prompt: A lung tumor measuring 2.6 centimeters with involvement of a single ipsilateral mediastinal lymph node and no metastases corresponds to which stage?
A patient undergoing general anesthesia for lymph node sampling near the upper trachea requires airway management. What is most appropriate?
Multiple ipsilateral mediastinal lymph node stations are involved with malignancy. What is the classification?
A patient has a lung mass greater than 3 centimeters with negative imaging of lymph nodes. What is the best next step?
A patient with suspected lung cancer and mediastinal lymph node involvement undergoes a procedure with rapid on-site evaluation available. What is the best approach?
During staging, malignant cells are found in one mediastinal lymph node and another node is enlarged. What is the next step?
Which approach best balances diagnostic yield, safety, and molecular testing capability?
When should mediastinal cryobiopsy be used?
Which lymph node is most likely to yield adequate material for comprehensive molecular testing?
What is the most appropriate modern biomarker strategy in lung cancer evaluation?

options.label: Replace tissue sampling with plasma testing
Use a multimodal approach combining clinical and molecular data
Use biomarkers only for postoperative monitoring
